description_long: This object consists of several dead or leafless trees with exposed roots and sparse, brittle branches. The trunks are tall, thin, and mostly straight, with some slight bends and irregularities, and the bark appears dark and weathered. The branches are few, thin, and mostly clustered near the upper portions of the trunks, giving the trees a skeletal appearance. The roots are prominently visible, sprawling outward in twisted, gnarled forms, suggesting the trees may have been uprooted or are growing in eroded soil. There are also a few broken or fallen branches and root fragments scattered nearby. The overall look is stark and desolate, evoking a sense of decay or a post-wildfire environment.
description: A group of tall, dead or leafless trees with exposed, gnarled roots and sparse, brittle branches. The trunks are dark, thin, and weathered, with scattered broken branches and root fragments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the trees, their exposed roots, and several broken branches and root fragments on the ground.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thinness of the trunks and the outward sprawl of the roots, with the skeletal branches visible in profile.
description_view_2: Rear view emphasizing the straightness of the trunks and the sparse, upward-reaching branches, with less visibility of the root spread.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the length of the main trunk, the gnarled roots, and the scattered branch fragments.
